# Investing Basics

<br>

A short tour of the concepts behind every investment decision.

## Compounding

Compounding is interest earning interest. Over long horizons, it does the
heavy lifting.

```{r}
#| label: compounding-r
years   <- 0:40
balance <- 10000 * (1.07)^years
plot(years, balance, type = "l",
     xlab = "Years", ylab = "Balance ($)",
     main = "$10,000 at 7% compounded annually")
```

```{python}
#| label: compounding-py
years = list(range(0, 41))
balance = [10000 * (1.07) ** y for y in years]
balance[-1]  # ending balance after 40 years
```

## Risk vs. return

Higher expected return = higher expected volatility. Choose a level you can
**stick with** through downturns, not just one that looks attractive on a
spreadsheet.

## Diversification

Don't bet on one company, one sector, or one country. Broad index funds
make this nearly free.
